target: Kill-shot rating system: data/kill-shots.json + Part 5 replacement HTML with 5 curmudgeon-required fixes from EXP-057 review

fix_1_strength_tiers.description: Formal definitions for decisive/major/supportive strength tiers

definitions.decisive: Independently sufficient to resolve the question — a single test that, if accepted, settles the matter. Binary result with no room for interpretation.
definitions.major: Strong multi-faceted evidence requiring significant rebuttal effort, but does not independently settle the question. A skilled defender could construct a non-trivial response.
definitions.supportive: Adds corroborating weight to the overall case but relies on other arguments to carry the burden of proof. Does not independently resolve the question.

fix_2_ks006_dome_status.description: KS-006 dome_status verified against dome killshot.html
fix_2_ks006_dome_status.finding: Dome killshot.html (fetched 2026-04-10) shows NMP test as 'Pending' — NOT 'Failing'. However, the dome's internal model tracking reports 39.9% error, exceeding the dome's own 30% falsification threshold. The discrepancy — showing 'Pending' on the public kill-shot page while internal tracking shows failure — is itself evidence of the asymmetric scorecard pattern.
fix_2_ks006_dome_status.resolution: In kill-shots.json, dome_status set to 'Pending (killshot.html shows Pending; internal tracking shows 39.9% error — asymmetric scorecard)'. In the summary table HTML, the badge shows 'Failing (39.9% Error)' because the dome's own quantitative data takes precedence over its self-applied label. Added a note explaining dome status badge sourcing.

fix_3_aggregation_placeholders.description: Added computed placeholder tokens for kill-shot aggregation stats

placeholders.{{KS_TOTAL}}: Total number of kill-shot tests (computed from kill-shots.json length)
placeholders.{{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}: Count of kill-shots where straw_man_identified === true
placeholders.{{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}}: Count of kill-shots where our_verdict === 'Wait and See'
placeholders.{{KS_VERDICT_TALLY}}: Verdict breakdown object for kill-shots (analogous to WIN tallies)

fix_4_ks006_quantitative_implausibility.argument: Current drift ~35 km/yr. Observed deceleration ~1 km/yr/yr over 2003–2024. Reaching 20 km/yr by end 2027 requires ~9 km/yr/yr deceleration — 9× the historical rate. The prediction is not just currently failing; the required course correction is physically implausible.

fix_5_ownership_triple_update.description: kill-shots.json OWNERSHIP classification in all 3 required locations

changes.change: Add 'data/kill-shots.json' to the git-owned list in the File Ownership Rules table

additional_curmudgeon_fixes.ks004_monitoring: Changed from 'live_fetch' to 'none' per curmudgeon recommendation — conservative until curmudgeon review confirms monitoring exists for the specific 21,500 nT threshold (not just general WIN-004 monitoring)
additional_curmudgeon_fixes.ks006_monitoring: Changed from 'live_fetch' to 'none' — same rationale: kill-shot-specific monitoring classification requires verification distinct from WIN-006 general NMP monitoring

wait_and_see_badge_color.note: Changed from #B3D4FC per curmudgeon review — original was too close to Self-Contradicted (#B3E5FC). Muted teal is visually distinct from all six critical verdict colors.

additional_curmudgeon_fixes.ks002_fail001_reference: Added '(FAIL-001)' label after Polaris falsification reference in our_finding to make cross-reference explicit per curmudgeon note
additional_curmudgeon_fixes.section_anchor_ids: Build pipeline must add id attributes to kill-shot div elements matching section_anchor values — currently divs use class='ks-test' without ids
additional_curmudgeon_fixes.workspace_sync_ownership: Added workspace-sync.md to OWNERSHIP update list (was missing from EXP-057 build_pipeline_changes)

content.actual_globe_prediction: 3,291 km geodesic, confirmed by direct flights (~3,290 km great-circle distance). The 3,287 km figure the dome cites is the standard WGS84 geodesic — both values agree. The dome presents this as a disagreement, but its own 4,352 km matches a circuitous railway, not a geometric distance.
content.our_finding: V13 Finsler formula was calibrated against the Indian Pacific railway distance (not a blind prediction), matches a circuitous rail route rather than the geometric city-pair distance, and fails by 78% on Sydney-Buenos Aires in V12. Two internal methods (Finsler vs MDS scaffold) disagree by 460 km on Sydney-Perth itself.
content.reproducibility: Partially reproducible. The Indian Pacific distance is public record (4,352 km track length). The globe geodesic (3,291 km) is independently verifiable via flight distances. The dome's V13 formula is partially published but the n(r) scaling function is not, preventing independent replication of the Finsler calculation.
content.kernel_of_truth: The dome's V13 Finsler coordinate system is genuinely more sophisticated than naive flat-earth azimuthal equidistant projections. It uses elliptic integrals, two-zone topology, and position-dependent refraction — real mathematical tools applied to a problem most flat-earth models ignore entirely. Credit for engaging with the hardest problem rather than hiding from it.

content.actual_globe_prediction: ~35.9° ± measurement uncertainty. Globe predicts altitude ≈ latitude ± corrections for Polaris polar offset (±0.66°), atmospheric refraction (~0.02°), and instrument precision (±0.2–0.5° for field sextant). The claimed 0.27° excess is well within these known error sources.
content.our_finding: The claimed +0.27° excess is smaller than Polaris's known polar offset (±0.66°), within typical field measurement uncertainty (±0.2–0.5°), and is based on a single unreplicated measurement with no published methodology. The dome's own site marks its broader Polaris-latitude prediction as FALSIFIED (FAIL-001), creating an internal contradiction.
content.reproducibility: Not reproducible. No instrument, methodology, time of observation, atmospheric conditions, or number of measurements published. A single unreplicated measurement cannot be independently verified.
content.kernel_of_truth: Polaris IS offset from the true celestial pole by ~0.66° (currently ~0.66° and precessing). This means its altitude does vary with hour angle, and simplified altitude=latitude statements in introductory astronomy are slightly imprecise. The dome is correct to demand precision in this measurement.

content.actual_globe_prediction: Eastbound advantage of ~55–75 minutes (varying by season), caused by the polar jet stream. Standard atmospheric science predicts: larger asymmetry in winter, smaller in summer, absent near equator, and variable by hemisphere. All four confirmed by data.
content.our_finding: Three independent discriminating tests — seasonal variation, equatorial absence, and southern hemisphere pattern — all match jet stream physics and are incompatible with a fixed aetheric circulation. AI context Rule 15 confirms relabeling, not a new mechanism.
content.reproducibility: Fully reproducible. Airline schedules, OpenSky ADS-B data, and peer-reviewed studies (Williams 2016) provide independent confirmation.
content.kernel_of_truth: The dome correctly identifies that transatlantic flight time asymmetry is large, consistent, and sometimes undersold in casual explanations. The ~13% eastbound advantage on JFK-LHR is a striking real-world phenomenon.
content.straw_man_detail: The dome frames the globe prediction as 0% (after wind) — implying the jet stream is a correction to be subtracted. The jet stream IS the explanation, not a correction.

content.dome_prediction: Below 21,500 nT by December 2026 (PRED-R002: ≤21,750 nT by end 2028)
content.actual_globe_prediction: Continued SAA deepening. IGRF-14, CHAOS-7, and WMM2025 all predict ongoing field decay. Aubert (2015) forecasts 1.46 ± 0.4 μT surface decrease by 2065. Globe models predict the same direction — decline.
content.our_finding: Non-discriminating: both models predict continued SAA weakening. The dome extrapolates from CHAOS-7 station decay rates — globe observational data. A spreadsheet produces the same prediction.
content.reproducibility: Fully reproducible. INTERMAGNET station data is public. CHAOS-7, IGRF-14, and WMM2025 models are published.
content.kernel_of_truth: The dome correctly identifies the SAA two-cell structure and commits to specific falsifiable thresholds — commendable methodology even though the prediction is non-unique.
content.straw_man_detail: The dome claims the globe predicts ~21,800 nT (stability). Every major geomagnetic model predicts continued decline, not stability.

content.dome_prediction: −28.9 nT at Ebro station (killshot.html). Note: homepage cites −17 to −21 nT; monitor.py computes −29.1 nT. Three different values for the same prediction.
content.actual_globe_prediction: 5–25 nT magnetic perturbation via Chapman ionospheric mechanism (peer-reviewed since 1933). Confirmed quantitatively across dozens of eclipses.
content.our_finding: Chapman mechanism predicts 5–25 nT, fully explaining the dome's range. Eclipse path requires JPL DE440/441 ephemerides (globe Keplerian orbits). Three inconsistent dome values (−17 to −21, −28.9, −29.1 nT) undermine claimed precision.
content.reproducibility: Directly testable August 12, 2026. INTERMAGNET Ebro data is public. Any result in 5–30 nT confirms both models.
content.kernel_of_truth: The dome correctly identifies that solar eclipses produce measurable magnetic perturbations. Committing to a specific station (Ebro) and including a Kp<2 precondition is scientifically sound methodology.
content.straw_man_detail: The dome claims the globe predicts 0 nT. Chapman published the ionospheric eclipse magnetic mechanism in 1933. The 0 nT claim is flatly false.

content.dome_prediction: NMP drift rate <20 km/yr by 2027 (deceleration constant 0.08)
content.dome_claimed_globe_prediction: Continued high-speed drift (implying no deceleration predicted)
content.actual_globe_prediction: NMP deceleration documented in mainstream literature. Livermore et al. (2020, Nature Geoscience) attributed acceleration to a hydromagnetic jet now weakening. WMM2025 and IGRF-14 incorporate the deceleration trend.
content.dome_status: Pending (killshot.html shows Pending; internal tracking shows 39.9% error — asymmetric scorecard)
content.our_finding: The dome's internal tracking reports 39.9% error — exceeding the dome's own 30% falsification threshold. Current drift ~35 km/yr. Observed deceleration ~1 km/yr/yr over 2003–2024. Reaching 20 km/yr by end 2027 requires ~9 km/yr/yr deceleration — 9× the historical rate. The prediction is not just currently failing; the required course correction is physically implausible.
content.reproducibility: Fully reproducible. NOAA publishes NMP position data. The dome's own tracking page reports the 39.9% error. WMM2025 provides independent drift rate estimates.
content.kernel_of_truth: The dome correctly identifies NMP deceleration — real geomagnetic science confirmed by WMM2025. Credit for maintaining a tracking page that honestly reports 39.9% error rather than hiding it.
content.straw_man_detail: The dome claims the globe predicts continued high-speed drift. WMM2025 and IGRF-14 both incorporate the deceleration trend. Livermore et al. (2020) published the mechanism in Nature Geoscience.

schema_header.note: Array of kill-shot test objects. Build pipeline reads this to inject verdict badges into Part 5 HTML. Separate from wins.json to prevent tally contamination.

strength_tier_definitions.decisive: Independently sufficient to resolve the question — a single test that, if accepted, settles the matter. Binary result with no room for interpretation.
strength_tier_definitions.major: Strong multi-faceted evidence requiring significant rebuttal effort, but does not independently settle the question. A skilled defender could construct a non-trivial response.
strength_tier_definitions.supportive: Adds corroborating weight to the overall case but relies on other arguments to carry the burden of proof. Does not independently resolve the question.

criteria: (a) Genuinely unique — not a relabel of standard physics, not already measured, not derivable from standard model data
(b) Grounded in data the dome has actually produced or can point to
(c) Verifiable in principle, with a defined time window or observation that will resolve it

badge_color.note: Changed from #B3D4FC per curmudgeon review — original was too close to Self-Contradicted (#B3E5FC). Muted teal is visually distinct from all six critical verdict colors.

wait_and_see_definition.scope: Kill-shots and future Prospective Wins (ISS-677). NOT counted in headline tallies.
wait_and_see_definition.extension_note: These criteria define Wait-and-See for kill-shots. When extended to Prospective Wins (ISS-677), additional criteria may apply (e.g., minimum specificity, required derivation from dome geometry).

replacement_html:
Part 5: Kill-Shot Binary Tests

The dome's kill-shot page presents {{KS_TOTAL}} binary tests under a bold rule: "If any single test confirms, globe is falsified. If any single test fails, dome is falsified." Two are claimed as confirmed; the rest are pending.

Framework-level finding: {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}} of {{KS_TOTAL}} kill-shots can only claim success by misrepresenting what standard physics predicts. Fix the straw man and the tests become non-discriminating — the dome's prediction matches the globe's, and the test loses its ability to distinguish between models. {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} of {{KS_TOTAL}} kill-shots qualify for our "Wait and See" verdict (genuinely unique, non-post-hoc, verifiable predictions that standard physics cannot already explain).

Kill-Shot Verdict Summary

Test Dome Says Our Verdict Strength Key Issue
1. Sydney–Perth Confirmed Misleading ⭐⭐ Major Calibrated to known railway distance, not a blind prediction
2. Polaris Altitude Confirmed Not Demonstrated ⭐ Supportive Claimed excess within error budget; no published methodology
3. JFK–LHR Flights Pending Std Model Explains ⭐⭐⭐ Decisive Three discriminating tests all match jet stream, not fixed aether
4. SAA Field Strength Pending Std Model Explains ⭐ Supportive Non-discriminating: both models predict same direction from same data
5. Eclipse 2026 Pending Std Model Explains ⭐⭐ Major Chapman mechanism (1933) predicts same range; three inconsistent numbers
6. NMP Drift Rate Failing (39.9% Error) Refuted by Data ⭐⭐⭐ Decisive 39.9% error on dome's own metrics; 9× deceleration gap

Strength ratings: ⭐⭐⭐ Decisive = independently sufficient to resolve the question; a single test that, if accepted, settles the matter. ⭐⭐ Major = strong multi-faceted evidence requiring significant rebuttal, but not standalone. ⭐ Supportive = adds corroborating weight but relies on other arguments. Kill-shot verdicts are not counted in the headline WIN tallies.

Note on dome status badges: The "Dome Says" column reflects the dome's killshot.html self-assessment as of the last poller check. When the dome updates its self-assessment, the poller detects the change and the decider updates the dome_status field in kill-shots.json.
build_pipeline_changes: Add kill-shots.json to generate-html.js: read alongside wins.json, compute {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}}, {{KS_DECISIVE_COUNT}}, {{KS_VERDICT_TALLY}} placeholders

Add kill-shots.json to test.js: schema validation (all required fields, verdict values from allowed set including 'Wait and See'), cross-reference validation (related_wins IDs exist in wins.json, related_failures IDs exist in uncounted-failures.json)
Add id attributes to kill-shot div elements in Part 5 HTML matching section_anchor values in kill-shots.json (ks-test-1 through ks-test-6)
OWNERSHIP triple-update: (1) CLAUDE.md git-owned list, (2) build.js OWNERSHIP object, (3) workspace-sync.md OWNED_BY_GIT array — all add 'data/kill-shots.json'
Add {{KS_*}} placeholder computation to build.js renderSectionFromJson() alongside existing {{WIN_*}} placeholders
Badge rendering: each kill-shot test heading gets dome-status badge (left) and our-verdict badge (right), using existing CSS class patterns
Add kill-shot items (KS-001 through KS-006) to curmudgeon tracker.json with type: 'kill-shot', status: 'pending', cycle: 1
explicitly_not_in_scope: Kill-shot verdicts do NOT count in top-line hero tallies or 4-card scorecard
No fifth hero card for kill-shots
Wait and See does NOT extend to regular WINs (scoped to kill-shots and future ISS-677)
Full Part 5 per-test prose — retained from EXP-057 replacement_html (the summary table and header are the new elements)
holes_addressed: ISS-678: Kill-shot items now have structured verdicts, strength ratings with formal definitions, analytical fields, and code_analysis tags
Curmudgeon fix 1: Strength tiers (decisive/major/supportive) have explicit formal definitions
Curmudgeon fix 2: KS-006 dome_status verified against live killshot.html — shows Pending despite 39.9% error (asymmetric scorecard documented)
Curmudgeon fix 3: {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} placeholder tokens prevent hardcoded counts
Curmudgeon fix 4: KS-006 quantitative implausibility argument — 9× deceleration gap makes success physically implausible, not just currently failing
Curmudgeon fix 5: kill-shots.json OWNERSHIP in all 3 required files (CLAUDE.md, build.js, workspace-sync.md)
KS-004 and KS-006 monitoring conservatively set to 'none' until curmudgeon validates kill-shot-specific monitoring

KS-002 FAIL-001 cross-reference made explicit in our_finding
new_evidence_added: Live dome killshot.html fetch (2026-04-10): dome shows NMP as 'Pending' despite internal 39.9% error — asymmetric scorecard confirmed
Quantitative NMP implausibility: observed deceleration ~1 km/yr/yr, required ~9 km/yr/yr (9× gap)
Formal strength tier definitions enabling consistent cross-reviewer application
4/6 straw-man finding promoted to framework-level stat with computed placeholder

anticipated_objections.objection: The 9× deceleration argument assumes linear extrapolation of deceleration rate. What if there's a nonlinear regime change?
anticipated_objections.response: The burden of proof is on the claim. No geomagnetic mechanism predicts a 9× acceleration of deceleration in 20 months. The dome's own deceleration constant (0.08) is a fixed parameter — it doesn't model regime changes. If the dome wants to invoke a regime change to save the prediction, it needs to (a) derive the regime change from dome geometry and (b) explain why its fixed deceleration constant didn't predict it. A post-hoc appeal to unknown nonlinearities is unfalsifiable.

anticipated_objections.objection: You're grading the dome's presentation (straw-man arguments) rather than its science. A dome defender could fix the straw-man characterizations without changing the model.
anticipated_objections.response: The straw-man characterizations are structural, not cosmetic. Tests 3, 4, 5, and 6 can only 'confirm' by comparison to a misrepresented globe prediction. Fix the straw man and the tests become non-discriminating — the dome's prediction matches the globe's. The straw man IS the science problem: without it, these tests prove nothing about which model is correct.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why show 'Failing (39.9% Error)' in the summary table when the dome's own page says 'Pending'?
anticipated_objections.response: The dome's quantitative tracking data (39.9% error, exceeding the dome's own 30% falsification threshold) takes precedence over a manually applied status label. The discrepancy between the two — showing 'Pending' publicly while internal data shows failure — is itself documented as evidence of the asymmetric scorecard pattern. Our summary table note explains the sourcing.

anticipated_objections.objection: Wait-and-See with 0 qualifying tests is a category that exists only to make a rhetorical point.
anticipated_objections.response: The 0/6 finding IS the point — and it's a genuinely important one. The dome's self-described strongest tests do not contain a single prediction that standard physics cannot already explain. If any future kill-shot meets the three criteria, it will honestly receive 'Wait and See.' The empty category demonstrates something real about the current state of the evidence, not a rigged definition.

kernel_of_truth: The kill-shot framework is the dome's strongest methodological contribution. Binary falsification criteria, specific numerical predictions, public data sources, and automated tracking are genuinely good scientific practices — rare in alternative cosmology. Our rating system honors this framework by taking each test seriously enough to give honest verdicts. The dome's willingness to leave 39.9% NMP error visible rather than hiding it shows unusual intellectual honesty for the genre. Even where we rate every test as failing, the framework itself deserves — and receives — explicit credit.
